questions.text: Provide a brief description of your business or organisation, including any commercial relationships you have with TWG operated dealerships, or Sharton Group operated dealerships.

Outline any concerns regarding the impact of the Acquisition on competition, including if any of the Sharton Motors sites are close competitors with TWG sites, or if there is only a small number of competing dealership groups in the area.
Provide any additional information or comments that you consider relevant to the ACCC’s assessment of the Acquisition.

Identify the dealerships you compete against (at the dealership group or OEM brand level). Specify the OEM brands that each competitor represents. As part of your response, explain what makes them strong competitors to you (e.g. price, range, proximity, service levels).

For any new car dealerships you operate in the Newcastle/Hunter Valley region, specify the distances or areas your customers travel to purchase a new car. If known, please provide percentages of customers travelling 0- 10km, 10-20km, 20-30km and 30-40km to purchase a new car.
To what extent do dealerships offering different OEM brands compete? Which brands compete against each of the Toyota and GWM/Haval brands?

If your dealership offers servicing and repairs, to what extent do you compete with Independent Repairers in the supply of these services? Explain how the level of competition may differ for (i) cars within OEM warranty periods (ii) cars outside OEM warranty periods, and (iii) cars with capped price servicing.

For any servicing and repair sites you operate in the Newcastle/Hunter Valley region, specify the distances or areas your customers travel for car servicing and repairs. If known, please provide percentages of customers travelling 0- 10km, 10-20km, 20-30km and 30-40km to obtain car servicing and repairs. If you do not operate any car servicing and repair sites in the area, please respond to the above based on other sites you operate in surrounding areas.
